---
name: output-template
description: Output template for the fix-identified-ats-issues skill. Defines the structure and formatting of the ATS Fix Guide document produced by the skill.
---

# Output Template — ATS Fix Guide

Use this template to format the ATS Fix Guide produced by Step 7 of the `fix-identified-ats-issues` skill.

---

## Document Header

```markdown
# ATS Fix Guide

**Resume:** [original filename, e.g. Resume - Franck BOULLIER-Jul-2025.pdf]
**Job Title:** [job title extracted from the job description]
**ATS Score Report:** [filename if provided by user, or "Generated by ats-score-simulator skill"]
**Fix Guide Date:** [today's date, YYYY-MM-DD]

---

**Current ATS Score:** [overall score from the ATS Score Report, e.g. 58/100 — Weak match]
**Estimated Score After All Fixes:** [score range from the ATS Score Report, e.g. 72–78%]

---
```

---

## How to Use This Guide Section

```markdown
## How to Use This Guide

Each fix below includes:

- **Before** — the exact text currently in your resume (or `(not present in resume)` for new additions), shown in a `text` code block for easy copying.
- **After** — the replacement or addition text, ready to paste directly into your resume, shown in a `text` code block.
- **Rationale** — why this specific change improves your ATS score and by how much.

Apply fixes in order: **Critical → High → Medium → Low**.  
You do not need to apply every fix — start with Critical and High Priority items for the greatest score gain.
```

---

## Fix Sections

Fixes are grouped by priority, matching the ATS Score Report's Improvement Plan. Within each priority group, fixes are numbered sequentially (C1, C2 … for Critical; H1, H2 … for High; M1, M2 … for Medium; L1, L2 … for Low).

### Critical Fixes Template

```markdown
---

## Critical Fixes

> These fixes have the **highest impact** on your ATS score. Address all of them before applying.

---

### Fix C1: [Short descriptive title, e.g. "Add API and Embedded Finance keywords to Professional Summary"]

**Section:** [Resume section affected, e.g. Professional Summary / Work Experience – UNIQGIFT / Skills]  
**ATS Dimension:** Keyword Match (35% weight)

**Before:**

```text
[Exact verbatim text copied from the resume. Preserve original punctuation, casing, line breaks.
If the section does not exist at all, write: (not present in resume)]
```

**After:**

```text
[Complete replacement or new text, ready to paste. Write the full element — entire paragraph,
entire bullet point, entire section — not just the changed words.]
```

**Rationale:**  
[2–4 sentences explaining: (1) which ATS scoring dimension improves, (2) why the specific wording
change achieves this, (3) how significant the impact is.]

---

### Fix C2: [Short descriptive title]

**Section:** [section name]  
**ATS Dimension:** [e.g. Experience Tenure (27% weight)]

**Before:**

```text
[Exact text]
```

**After:**

```text
[Replacement text]
```

**Rationale:**  
[2–4 sentences]

---
```

### High Priority Fixes Template

```markdown
---

## High Priority Fixes

> Apply these after all Critical fixes. They produce meaningful score improvements.

---

### Fix H1: [Short descriptive title]

**Section:** [section name]  
**ATS Dimension:** [dimension + weight]

**Before:**

```text
[Exact text or (not present in resume)]
```

**After:**

```text
[Complete replacement or addition]
```

**Rationale:**  
[2–4 sentences]

---
```

### Medium Priority Fixes Template

```markdown
---

## Medium Priority Fixes

> Apply these after Critical and High fixes. They provide incremental score gains.

---

### Fix M1: [Short descriptive title]

**Section:** [section name]  
**ATS Dimension:** [dimension + weight]

**Before:**

```text
[Exact text or (not present in resume)]
```

**After:**

```text
[Complete replacement or addition]
```

**Rationale:**  
[2–4 sentences]

---
```

### Low Priority Fixes Template

```markdown
---

## Low Priority Fixes

> Optional polish items. Address these last for a well-rounded, fully optimized resume.

---

### Fix L1: [Short descriptive title]

**Section:** [section name]  
**ATS Dimension:** [dimension + weight]

**Before:**

```text
[Exact text or (not present in resume)]
```

**After:**

```text
[Complete replacement or addition]
```

**Rationale:**  
[2–4 sentences]

---
```

---

## Estimated Score After Fixes Section

```markdown
---

## Estimated Score After Fixes

| Fixes applied | Estimated ATS score | ATS outcome |
|---|---|---|
| Current score | [e.g. 58%] | [e.g. Weak match] |
| Critical fixes only | [e.g. 64–68%] | [e.g. Weak → Moderate match] |
| Critical + High Priority | [e.g. 72–78%] | [e.g. Moderate match] |
| All fixes applied | [e.g. 78–82%] | [e.g. Moderate → Strong match] |

**Main remaining gap after all fixes:**  
[1–2 sentences describing the structural gap that cannot be closed by text changes alone,
e.g. years of domain-specific experience the candidate genuinely does not have.]

**Note on honesty:**  
All "After" suggestions reflect only experience, skills, and qualifications already present in
your resume. No fix fabricates or exaggerates your background.
```

---

## Worked Example

Below is a worked example based on a real ATS Score Report to illustrate how the template is applied.

---

### Example: Fix C1 (worked)

**Context:** The ATS Score Report identified "API / APIs / Application Programming Interfaces" as a Critical missing keyword (Priority: Critical). The resume had no mention of APIs anywhere.

```markdown
### Fix C1: Add API and Embedded Finance keywords to the opening summary

**Section:** Professional Summary (opening tagline / first paragraph)  
**ATS Dimension:** Keyword Match (35% weight)

**Before:**

```text
Experienced business leader with 20+ years of experience in digital transformation,
FinTech, data platforms, and business development across APAC and global markets.
```

**After:**

```text
Senior technology and product leader with 20+ years of experience in digital transformation,
API-driven platforms, and Embedded Finance solutions. Proven track record building and scaling
FinTech and B2B digital products across APAC and global markets, with deep expertise in
data platforms, cloud infrastructure, and Open Banking ecosystems.
```

**Rationale:**  
The job description lists "API", "Embedded Finance", and "Open Banking" as Critical keywords.
The current summary contains none of these terms, resulting in a near-zero Keyword Match score
for the most heavily weighted ATS dimension (35%). Adding these keywords to the summary — the
section the ATS parser weights most heavily — closes the single largest gap in the keyword score.
The rewrite preserves factual accuracy by using "API-driven platforms" and "Open Banking
ecosystems" as accurate descriptors of UNIQGIFT's platform work.
```

---

### Example: Fix H1 (worked)

**Context:** The ATS Score Report identified non-standard section headings ("Seasoned Executive", "Entrepreneur", "Other Roles") as a High Priority formatting issue.

```markdown
### Fix H1: Replace non-standard experience headings with "Professional Experience"

**Section:** Work Experience section headings  
**ATS Dimension:** Formatting & Completeness (10–15% weight)

**Before:**

```text
## Seasoned Executive
...
## Entrepreneur
...
## Other Roles
```

**After:**

```text
## Professional Experience
*(All roles listed in reverse-chronological order under a single "Professional Experience"
section heading. Remove "Seasoned Executive", "Entrepreneur", and "Other Roles" sub-headings.)*
```

**Rationale:**  
ATS parsers identify experience sections by looking for standard anchor headings such as
"Professional Experience", "Work Experience", or "Employment History". Non-standard headings
like "Seasoned Executive" cause the parser to either misclassify entire role groups or skip
them, directly reducing keyword and tenure scores. Consolidating all roles under one standard
heading ensures the parser correctly maps every job entry to the Experience field.
```

---

## Naming Convention

Save the output file as:

```
ATS-Fix-Guide-[CANDIDATE-LAST-NAME]-[Job-Title-Slug].md
```

Examples:

- `ATS-Fix-Guide-BOULLIER-Head-API-Embedded-Finance.md`
- `ATS-Fix-Guide-SMITH-Senior-Data-Engineer.md`
- `ATS-Fix-Guide-NGUYEN-Product-Manager-Payments.md`
